title: Running Python Code
summary: Use the ZoeSkoul browser code editor, Run button, and output panel to execute Python code.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Running Python Code
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In this topic, you'll learn how to run Python code using the ZoeSkoul browser code editor. You'll see how to print messages and understand the basic syntax of Python. For example, to print a message, you can use the `print()` function:

```python
print('Hello, World!')
```

Try it yourself by typing the code in the editor and clicking Run!

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Syntax Errors
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Syntax errors occur when the code does not follow the correct format. For example, if you forget to close a parenthesis in a print statement:

```python
print('Hello, World!'
```

This will raise a syntax error. Make sure to check your code for any missing symbols. Your turn: Fix the syntax error in the example above!

quizDraft.title: What does the print function do?
options: It outputs text to the screen.
It saves the code to a file.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what happens when you want to display information.

help.concept: The print function is used to display output to the user.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that describes displaying text.
help.hint_2: Remember, print is not for saving or running code.

quizDraft.title: Which of the following are valid print statements?
quizDraft.hint: Check for proper syntax in each option.

help.concept: Valid print statements must have correct syntax and quotation marks for strings.
help.hint_1: Look for options that include quotes around text.
help.hint_2: Numbers can be printed without quotes.

quizDraft.title: Order the steps to run Python code.
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the steps in the correct order.
quizDraft.hint: Think about the logical order of the steps.

help.concept: This task is about understanding the sequence behind order the steps to run python code..
help.hint_1: Find the step that must happen first before the others make sense.
help.hint_2: Check which action depends on a previous step already being completed.

quizDraft.title: Arrange the components of a print statement.
quizDraft.prompt: Put the parts of the print statement in the correct order.
quizDraft.hint: Read each piece and arrange them in the order the statement should be understood.

help.concept: The pieces should form a valid statement in a logical order.
help.hint_1: Start with the piece that introduces the idea or action.
help.hint_2: Place dependent pieces after the part they describe or complete.

help.hint_2: Choose the option that directly matches the question without relying on answer wording.

quizDraft.title: Fix the Syntax Error
